CVX.reasoning: 1. MACRO REGIME: Transitional — fragile risk-on with a critical energy-sector caveat. The Iran/Strait of Hormuz ceasefire has partially deflated the geopolitical risk premium that was driving oil above $100. Fed on hold at 4.25–4.50% with inflation re-accelerating from energy. This is a lower-energy-price regime relative to March peaks, and CVX's self-reported $2.7–3.7B Middle East earnings hit (per prior wiki thesis) means deteriorating fundamentals meet a deflating commodity tailwind. If the ceasefire holds and Brent stays below $100, energy sector inflows stall. 2. RELATIVE STRENGTH: CVX is a clear underperformer. Daily ROC: 5d +1.28%, 10d +0.72%, 21d -10.79%. The 21-day figure is severe — a 10.79% decline while the S&P hit all-time highs on April 27 signals sector outflows and stock-specific deterioration. Price is below daily EMA-20, EMA-21, and EMA-50. Daily ADX at 32.17 (strong) with -DI (28.59) > +DI (21.56) confirms bearish trend dominance. Daily OBV is in a downtrend and diverging from price — institutional distribution. 3. ASYMMETRY MATH (bearish entry on hourly exhaustion): Current intraday price $192.22 represents a countertrend bounce — hourly RSI is 70.25 (overbought), price is above the hourly Bollinger upper band (pct_b = 1.04), and a confirmed hourly bearish RSI divergence exists. This is the fade setup. Entry ~$192.00 on hourly rollover. Target $181.76 (nearest daily support, 12-test level) = 5.3% downside. Stop $195.18 (hourly fib ext 1.272 / recent hourly high area) = 1.6% risk. R/R = 5.3% / 1.6% = 3.3:1 — well above the 2:1 minimum. Stretch target $177.74 (recent daily low) = 7.4% / 1.6% = 4.6:1. 4. CAPITAL PRESERVATION: CVX has a strong balance sheet — leverage is not a flag. However, the combination of earnings headwinds (Middle East hit), OBV distribution, and a sector that thrives on high oil prices (which are now partially deflated by ceasefire) creates fundamental deterioration risk if Iran ceasefire holds. The regime shift risk is asymmetric: re-escalation spikes oil and hurts CVX via uncertainty overhang despite higher prices; sustained ceasefire removes the energy premium. Wiki thesis from prior run (Apr 11) placed a bear short entry at $192 with a $178 target — current price is nearly identical, thesis unchanged.

WMT.reasoning: 1. MACRO REGIME: Transitional, with a genuine WMT-specific tailwind. Consumer stress is mounting — core PCE at 2.7%, oil-driven CPI at multi-year highs, and household budgets under pressure. This is the textbook WMT trade-down environment: budget-stressed shoppers migrate from premium retailers to everyday-low-price. The macro is a tailwind for WMT's thesis, but the tailwind is already well-known and reflected in the valuation (52x earnings on ~2% revenue growth per wiki). 2. RELATIVE STRENGTH: WMT shows mixed signals. Daily EMA stack is aligned uptrend (confirmed TRUE) — all major MAs in proper bullish order. 21-day ROC of +3.82% vs. S&P at new highs suggests in-line to slight outperformance on the 21d basis, though the 5d ROC of -1.55% reflects a recent distribution phase (stock fell from $132.46 high to $127.59 over four sessions). Daily OBV trend is UP and not diverging — no institutional selling signal yet. ADX at 14.72 (weak trend) — the uptrend lacks conviction. RSI-14 at 50.87 is neutral. Hourly picture is bearish short-term: hourly -DI (27.82) > +DI (18.24) with ADX 26 = moderate bearish momentum intraday. Hourly RSI at 39.2 approaches oversold. 3. ASYMMETRY MATH: This is why the trade is a PASS at current levels. Current price $128.01. Nearest overhead resistance: $128.49 (22-test daily pivot high) = only +0.37% away — effectively zero room to run before hitting a wall. Next resistance: $132.46 (recent high) = +3.5%. Support: $127.33 (25-test hourly level) = -0.53%, then $125.91 (volume-confirmed hourly) = -1.6%, then $121.37 (daily) = -5.2%. A long entry here targeting $128.49 offers 0.37% upside vs. 1.6% downside = 0.23:1 — deeply negative asymmetry. To enter bullishly targeting $132.46, a trader needs a confirmed break and close above $128.49 on expanding volume, or a pullback entry to the $126-127 zone. Neither condition is met today. 4. CAPITAL PRESERVATION: WMT is a Mega-cap consumer staple — no balance sheet concerns. Volatility is low (ATR 2.03%). The risk is valuation compression: at 52x earnings, any miss on tariff cost pass-through or consumer confidence deterioration is a de-rating event. This is not a capital-preservation flag per se, but it caps the upside multiple expansion case. VERDICT: The durable WMT trade-down thesis is intact, the macro tailwind is real, and the daily trend structure is constructive. But the entry here — with $128.49 resistance 37 cents overhead — offers no actionable asymmetry. Neutral pending either a pullback to ~$126.50 (would give 3:1+ toward $132.46) or a confirmed volume breakout above $128.49.
